title: Project Manifest
description: Validates manifest.json for a generated project in /projects or /scratch. Used by scripts/validate.js and enforced in the Replit agent generation workflow.

name.description: Display name of the project. Title case. Used in index listings and PR titles.

slug.description: Filesystem-safe identifier. Lowercase, hyphen-separated, no leading/trailing hyphens. Must match the folder name without the date prefix (e.g., folder '2026-03-14-funnel-drop-tool' → slug 'funnel-drop-tool').

date_created.description: ISO 8601 date the project folder was first created. YYYY-MM-DD. Set once and not updated.

date_updated.description: ISO 8601 date of the last meaningful change to source, docs, or metadata. YYYY-MM-DD.

bucket.description: Which idea-matrix bucket this project belongs to. Use 'other' for concepts that originate outside the idea matrix.

persona.description: Specific description of the target user. Must name a role, context, and what they are trying to do — not a job title alone. E.g., 'PM at a B2B SaaS company reading an A/B test result without a statistician available' not 'product manager'.
examples: PM or growth analyst investigating an unexplained drop in a funnel step
Staff engineer or technical PM managing a design review and RFC process
PM coordinating a product launch across eng, marketing, sales, and support

problem_statement.description: The specific problem this project addresses. Must name a failure mode, not a domain. Should answer: what goes wrong, for whom, and why the existing approach is insufficient.
examples: A/B test dashboards show p-values but most PMs lack the statistical fluency to catch underpowered tests or multiple-comparisons problems before making a ship decision.
Win/loss interview notes contain patterns — objections, missing features, competitor mentions — but they are unstructured and nobody has time to read 50 deal summaries manually.

complexity.description: Estimated implementation complexity. simple = one focused UI, no auth, shippable in a day. intermediate = multiple views or states, some persistence or processing, 3–5 days. complex = real backend, data pipeline, or non-trivial algorithmic work, 1–2 weeks.

artifact_type.description: A plain-language description of what the finished artifact is. Not a category label — a description of the actual thing. Be specific about structure and interaction.
examples: Single-page paste-and-score tool with structured output sections and copy-to-clipboard
Two-pane web app: left pane accepts structured input, right pane renders annotated output with per-section callouts
CLI tool that reads a directory of CSV exports and writes a ranked HTML report
Multi-step wizard that generates a scoped checklist based on launch type and affected surfaces

style_direction.description: The visual identity applied to this project, selected from styles/style-matrix.yaml. Every project must use exactly one style. The implementation must follow the style spec — colors, typography, spacing, component personality, and motion level.

stack.description: Specific technologies used. Name the actual libraries and runtimes, not categories. Use the real package or tool name.

status.description: Lifecycle status of the project. published = cleared quality gate, lives in /projects. draft = in progress, lives in /scratch. rejected = failed quality gate with documented reason, stays in /scratch.

publish_recommendation.description: Quality gate output. publish = all hard fails clear, ready for /projects. hold = soft flags remain but no hard fails; needs specific fixes before publish. scratch = one or more hard fails; route to /scratch with reason documented in decision-log.md.

tags.description: Lowercase hyphenated tags for filtering and index search. At least one tag should reflect the primary persona action (e.g., 'prioritization', 'funnel-analysis', 'stakeholder-communication'). No generic tags: 'tool', 'utility', 'app', 'web' are not allowed.

description.description: One sentence describing what the project does. No trailing period. Written for a reader who has not yet clicked into the project — it must stand alone in an index listing.
examples: Scores a set of OKRs against a structured rubric and returns specific rewrite suggestions for each failing key result
Generates a prioritized hypothesis list for PMs investigating unexplained funnel drops, organized by category with explicit investigation steps

has_demo.description: Whether a live demo URL is available and working.

demo_url.description: URL to a live, publicly accessible demo. Must be null if has_demo is false. Must be a valid URI if has_demo is true.

screenshot.description: Relative path to the primary screenshot file within the project folder. Must be under screenshots/. Must be null only if the project has no UI and no visual output. Pattern: screenshots/[filename].[png|jpg|webp].

idea_matrix_id.description: The id field from prompts/idea-matrix.yaml that this project was built from. Null if the concept originated outside the idea matrix.

notes.description: Internal freeform notes visible to contributors but not rendered in public index views. Use for known limitations, follow-up ideas, or context about the publish_recommendation.

allOf.description: If has_demo is true, demo_url must be a non-null string.
If publish_recommendation is 'publish', status must be 'published' or 'draft'. A rejected project cannot be recommended for publish.
If status is 'published', publish_recommendation must be 'publish'. A project cannot be published if it was held or scratched.
If status is 'rejected', publish_recommendation must be 'scratch'.
